ops.title: Claude Code ≥2.1.91 puts plugin bin/ on Bash PATH, but shell snapshots pin the version — stale snapshots run old binaries silently
ops.body: Claude Code ≥ 2.1.91 adds each enabled plugin's root `bin/` to the Bash tool PATH (pure filesystem convention, no plugin.json field), so plugin CLIs run as bare commands from any cwd. The entry is version-pinned and baked into `~/.claude/shell-snapshots/*.sh` at session start: after a plugin update, existing sessions keep resolving the OLD binary — observed with cc-present 0.8.1 lingering across ~10 sessions after 0.9.0 was installed. So a bare plugin command that *answers* can still be stale; compare `<tool> --version` against the installed plugin version when behavior looks outdated, and fall back to `"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/<tool>"` (always the current install) or a fresh session. Also: `$CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` reaches Bash subprocesses (≥2.1.132), but no project-root env var does — CLIs needing the repo root must derive it from cwd. Related: [[cc-present-window-keyed-bare-invocation]].
